sources.excerpt: All-Purpose's owner site identifies AP Pizza Shop in Bethesda as a current location and links customers to its AP menu/ordering surface.
notes: Used for brand/location identity and the owner-controlled relationship to the Toast ordering menu.

sources.excerpt: The live restaurant-linked lunch surface publishes 46 products across Snacks + Salads, Fossette Focacceria, Deck-Oven Slices, Lunch Pies, Dippies, and Sweet Treats at AP Pizza Shop, 4747 Bethesda Avenue.
rowIdentifiers: AP Pizza Shop — 4747 Bethesda Avenue, Bethesda, MD
notes: The live Toast page and stable restaurant-linked item URLs are the authority for lunch product and ingredient claims.
The page publishes selected ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure.

sources.excerpt: The live restaurant-linked dinner surface publishes 35 products and identifies Duke #7, Pizza Kit, and Pizza Dough as dinner-only additions to the current 49-product lunch/dinner union.
rowIdentifiers: AP Pizza Shop — 4747 Bethesda Avenue, Bethesda, MD
Fossette Focacceria x AP Pizza Shop
notes: The live Toast page and stable restaurant-linked item URLs are the authority for dinner product and ingredient claims.
The Toast catalog changes by meal period; the lunch and dinner surfaces were reconciled as a union rather than treating one as stale.

sources.excerpt: Third-party transport snapshot of the restaurant-linked lunch menu with 46 product anchors and their Toast item URLs.
notes: This retained artifact is third-party transport corroboration only and is never relabeled as restaurant-linked or restaurant-issued evidence.
Its 46 product anchors were cross-checked against the independently read live restaurant-linked lunch surface.

sources.excerpt: Second third-party transport snapshot requested through the Duke #7 item URL; Toast returned the scheduled lunch surface rather than the dinner catalog.
notes: Despite the historical filename/source id, this artifact is not treated as a dinner snapshot; it corroborates Toast's meal-period routing behavior.
It remains third-party transport evidence and is excluded from all app source URLs.

sources.excerpt: Sydney Fish Market explains that whitebait appearing on Italian restaurant menus is called neonata; this supports interpreting AP Pizza Shop's explicitly listed neonata ingredient as fish.
rowIdentifiers: In Australia it’s often seen on Italian restaurant menus as ‘neonata’
notes: This third-party culinary reference establishes ingredient semantics only; it is not promoted to evidence that AP Pizza Shop officially publishes an allergen matrix.
The restaurant-linked Toast item remains the authority that The Tripper actually lists neonata.
